{"step_by_step_thinking": "The investigator wants to compare the mean peak flow of expiratory rates among different smoking categories (non-smokers, light smokers, moderate smokers, and heavy smokers). This scenario involves comparing the means of a continuous variable (peak flow) across more than two categorical groups (smoking status). One-way ANOVA (option A) is the appropriate statistical test for this purpose, as it assesses whether there are any statistically significant differences between the means of three or more independent (unrelated) groups. The other options are not suitable: a Student-t test is used for comparing two groups, a two-way ANOVA is used for analyzing the main effects and interaction between two factors, and a Chi-square test is used for categorical data, not continuous data like peak flow.", "answer_choice": "A"}
